3Create Debt Management Case: 
Debt Management
• In order to identify unpaid debts for each tax 
period, tax-type and taxpayer account, Trips+ 
maintains an ‘Allocation Balance’ on each posting.   
After defined periods, say daily, and for each 
individual tax-type, the query functionality 
detects non-filers by interrogating the taxpayer 
ledgers and automatically enforces the tax 
administration’s rules for late, non–filers, or 
payers. Examples of such functionality are the 
issuing of notices of escalating severity to the 
taxpayer by SMS, email, or letter. 
• Also at defined times debts are identified in the 
taxpayer ledger and prioritized according to the administration’s business rules (such as the 
amount of money at risk, the age of the debt and 
the entity/ sector) for debt management.  
• Once a taxpayer account is selected for debt 
management, the system automatically 
generates enforcement action in accordance 
with the authority’s business rules. For example, 
it can suspend the further issuance of notices, or 
generate either an assessment based on previous 
returns or a penalty, which are then posted to the 
account. Debt management and the ability to track and monitor 
taxpayer debts is a key function within Trips+.  It is 
essential for the support of revenue management, 
debt recovery, bankruptcy and liquidation 
proceedings, criminal prosecution of tax offences 
and the checking of tax debts required by other Government departments. The system therefore 
provides full case management functionality to 
support the work of the audit, objection and appeals 
and other departments.

4View Debt Management Case:


5Trips+ also the establishment of instalment agreements to repay debt, monitors their conformance and 
allows them to be cancelled should the taxpayer default. Authorized officials can write off debt in accordance 
with the authority’s business rules.
View Instalment Agreement:


6Once the weekly/monthly debt planning exercise is completed, authorized users can electronically allocate 
the case to a collector. A uniquely numbered case file is created and the collector can download the data that 
is required.
 
Update Debt Management Case:
• The system supports the collector to manage 
the case by, for example, by generating notices, 
enabling third party documents to be uploaded 
(such as court documents) and recording actions 
and outcomes during the collection exercise. 
 
• The functionality in Trips+ allows the 
identification, seizure, management and 
auctioning of a taxpayer’s assets.  The system 
interfaces with third party systems to identify 
the taxpayer’s assets.  When these are seized, 
the amount is recorded in the case file for 
final reconciliation when the debt is paid. The 
workflow also enforces rules regarding supervisor 
authorization, such as sending the case to a 
nominated supervisor for review and approval.• The performance measurement and business 
intelligence functionality enables all aspects 
of debt collection to be analyzed and reported. 
Examples include the analysis of debt by various 
attributes (such as age or tax-type) and how 
long it takes to collect particular types of debt by 
officer and region. The data can be presented in 
a number of formats such as dashboards, tables, 
electronic and hardcopy.
